text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence trained on massive amounts of text from the internet, books, and other sources. They work by predicting the most likely next word in a sentence based on patterns in their training data. While LLMs don't truly understand language, they can generate human-like responses to prompts. Users can shape these responses through prompt engineering, which involves carefully crafting instructions to get better results. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, or translate text, but they have limits—they can produce incorrect or biased information, and they lack real-world knowledge or feelings. They are useful tools, not perfect thinkers.
